value.rationale: Verification loops is a specific feature of Google's agentic coding product (Antigravity 2.0) that enables the agent to run local test commands (unit tests, build scripts) after making code changes and iterate automatically. This is a partial slice of agentic coding — specifically the test/build feedback loop aspect — rather than the full agentic coding capability. It surfaces in terminal/IDE contexts where code changes and test runs occur.
